headlines.title: Buildings collapse in Venezuelan capital after powerful earthquakes hit
headlines.snippet: Buildings collapsed in Venezuela's capital city following powerful earthquakes. The event caused significant structural damage in the area.

headlines.title: Two years on, Kenyan families still seek justice after Gen Z protests
headlines.snippet: Two years after Gen Z protests, Kenyan families are still seeking justice for those killed and missing. Investigations have reportedly stalled, leaving accountability elusive.

headlines.title: Venezuela struck by back-to-back earthquakes, many casualties feared
headlines.snippet: Venezuela was struck by consecutive powerful earthquakes, leading to fears of numerous casualties. The US Geological Survey indicated a high probability of significant loss of life.

headlines.title: Iran war live: US vows to defend Gulf interests; Israel kills 2 in Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The United States has pledged to defend Gulf interests amid ongoing conflict, while Israel reported the killing of two individuals in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Trump slams NATO over lax participation in Iran war in talk with Mark Rutte
headlines.snippet: Trump discussed NATO's participation in the Iran war with Mark Rutte. Rutte highlighted the use of US bases in Europe and reaffirmed support for the US military actions in Iran.

headlines.title: Trump criticises European allies over lack of support during war on Iran
headlines.snippet: Trump expressed criticism towards European allies for their perceived lack of support during the war on Iran. He stated that while US assistance wasn't needed, stronger backing would have been welcomed.

headlines.title: Trump casts doubt on US responsibility for deadly Iran school strike
headlines.snippet: Trump questioned the US involvement in a deadly school strike in Iran, stating he had seen no evidence of American responsibility. A Pentagon investigation into the incident is pending.

headlines.title: Vance says US reviewing conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase
headlines.snippet: Vance indicated that the US is reviewing the conditions for Türkiye's potential purchase of F-35 fighter jets. This review is to ensure compliance with American legal requirements before any transfer can proceed.

headlines.title: UN nuclear chief says inspections in Iran will go ahead despite Tehran's caution
headlines.snippet: The head of the IAEA confirmed that UN nuclear inspectors will proceed with inspections in Iran. This commitment remains despite Tehran's indication that such inspections would only occur after a final agreement.

headlines.title: Israeli army says it struck two people in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli army stated it struck two individuals in a vehicle within its area of occupation in southern Lebanon. The army claimed the individuals posed a threat to its forces.

headlines.title: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah killed in explosion in Yemen’s Mukalla
headlines.snippet: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah was killed in an explosion that occurred in Mukalla, Yemen.

headlines.title: Federal judge bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
headlines.snippet: A federal judge has issued an order preventing Trump from enforcing a requirement for proof of citizenship to vote.

headlines.title: US-Iran deal may leave Netanyahu as biggest casualty
headlines.snippet: A deal between the US and Iran is suggested to potentially leave Netanyahu as the most impacted figure.

headlines.title: Italy slams NATO chief’s comments on Iran war flights
headlines.snippet: Italy has publicly criticized statements made by the NATO chief concerning flights related to the Iran war.

headlines.title: Russian strike on Ukraine kills mine disposal expert: NGO
headlines.snippet: A Russian strike in Ukraine resulted in the death of a mine disposal expert, according to an NGO.

headlines.title: Trump seeks $88 bn in extra funding, mostly for Iran war
headlines.snippet: Trump has requested an additional $88 billion in funding, primarily allocated for the Iran war.

headlines.title: Colombia president-elect to work with Israel 'like never before'
headlines.snippet: The president-elect of Colombia has stated an intention to strengthen ties with Israel significantly.

headlines.title: UK government's answer to stolen Palestinian land? Ad watchdog
headlines.snippet: The UK government's response to the sale of stolen Palestinian land has been criticized as inadequate, focusing on an ad watchdog rather than stronger sanctions.

headlines.title: Syria trial opens for Assad cousin accused of war crimes
headlines.snippet: A trial has commenced in Syria for a cousin of Assad, who stands accused of committing war crimes.

headlines.title: US to progress with Turkey jet engine sales ahead of NATO summit
headlines.snippet: The United States is moving forward with sales of jet engines to Turkey. This development precedes an upcoming NATO summit.

headlines.title: مصدر أمني للميادين: تم تجهيز مقدمات جدول انسحاب الاحتلال من جنوب لبنان وهو قيد الدراسة
headlines.snippet: An security source indicated that preliminary plans for the Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon have been prepared and are currently under review.

headlines.title: ما بعد التفاهم الأمريكي الإيراني.. هل تتشكل ترتيبات إقليمية جديدة؟
headlines.snippet: Following the American-Iranian understanding, questions are being raised about the potential formation of new regional arrangements.

headlines.title: الدبلوماسية الإيرانية بعد تفاهم إسلام آباد: هل يُعاد تشكيل أمن الإقليم بعيداً عن واشنطن؟
headlines.snippet: The Iranian diplomacy following the Islamabad understanding raises questions about whether regional security is being reshaped independently of Washington.

headlines.title: تخبط داخلي في "إسرائيل" يعمق الأزمة وينعكس على الخارج... ما تفاصيل المشهد الإسرائيلي؟
headlines.snippet: Internal turmoil within Israel is exacerbating its crisis and reflecting on its external relations, with details of the Israeli situation being examined.

headlines.snippet: Iran has affirmed its commitment to dialogue and ending wars in the region, prompting questions about whether Washington and Tel Aviv will reciprocate.

headlines.title: 'Inhumane': Gaza flotilla activists recount Israeli detention ordeal
headlines.snippet: Activists from a Gaza flotilla recounted their experiences of detention by Israeli forces, describing the treatment as inhumane.

headlines.title: National exports rise 7.3%, trade deficit narrows 13.4% in first four months of 2026 — DoS
headlines.snippet: National exports increased by 7.3%, and the trade deficit decreased by 13.4% in the first four months of 2026, according to the Department of Statistics.

headlines.title: Crown Prince meets with CEOs of leading technology companies in Silicon Valley
headlines.snippet: The Crown Prince held meetings with chief executive officers from prominent technology companies in Silicon Valley.

headlines.title: Iran says deal to end Mideast war 'declaration of US defeat'
headlines.snippet: Iran has characterized the deal to end the Mideast war as a declaration of American defeat.

headlines.title: Mining sector expected to generate over 30,000 jobs by 2033 – ministry
headlines.snippet: The mining sector is projected to create over 30,000 jobs by the year 2033, according to the ministry.

headlines.title: Families of 4 missing post-cease-fire protest outside Grand Serail
headlines.snippet: Families of four individuals missing since a ceasefire protest gathered outside the Grand Serail.

headlines.title: Troops will not withdraw from Lebanon 'as long as I am PM,' Netanyahu says; Israel kills 2 in drone strike near Nabatieh
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that Israeli troops will not withdraw from Lebanon as long as he remains Prime Minister. Meanwhile, Israel killed two people in a drone strike near Nabatieh.

headlines.title: Washington talks stall on 2nd day over 'pilot zones'
headlines.snippet: Washington talks have stalled on their second day due to disagreements over 'pilot zones'.

headlines.title: AI could breach government and business defenses in months, US and its intelligence partners warn
headlines.snippet: An international alliance of intelligence agencies warned that AI could breach government and business defenses within months. The Five Eyes grouping urged proactive measures from leaders.

headlines.title: Israel deliberately targeting children in ongoing genocide against Palestinians, UN commission finds
headlines.snippet: A UN commission has found that Israeli forces are deliberately targeting children in Gaza, constituting genocide. The report details unprecedented death and injury.

headlines.title: WHO chief says Europe’s heat wave is putting health at risk
headlines.snippet: The WHO chief stated that Europe's heatwave is endangering public health and urged leaders to prioritize climate action. Temperatures in Europe are rising at twice the global average.

headlines.title: ‘Exploding oil?!’ The Middle East is about to find out
headlines.snippet: The Strait of Hormuz has reopened, and Middle Eastern countries are preparing to restart oil production. The implications for global oil supply are about to become clear.

headlines.snippet: Egypt's Prime Minister witnessed the signing of an MoU for the country's first wind turbine plant and a 2,000-megawatt wind farm project in the Gulf of Suez.

headlines.snippet: President Sheikh Mohamed received US Secretary of State Marco Rubio in Abu Dhabi.

headlines.snippet: Emirates' fleet of A380 aircraft will undergo urgent inspections due to the discovery of cracks in the wings.

headlines.snippet: UAE oil exports have recovered to 85% of pre-war levels, despite the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, according to the IEA.

headlines.snippet: Trump is scheduled to meet with munitions manufacturers as part of efforts to replenish weapons stockpiles.

headlines.title: IDF soldier found handcuffed in West Bank town
headlines.snippet: An IDF soldier was discovered handcuffed in a West Bank town. Preliminary investigations suggest the soldier was unarmed and had entered the West Bank with criminal intent.

headlines.title: Trump criticizes European NATO countries, praises Turkey's Erdogan in meeting with NATO Sec.-Gen.
headlines.snippet: Trump criticized European NATO countries while praising Turkey's Erdogan during a meeting with the NATO Secretary-General. He indicated he would likely approve fighter jet and engine sales to Turkey.

headlines.snippet: Trump referred to critics of Israel in New York's primaries as 'communists' after their electoral success.

headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that Israel will be the first to develop a solution to the global problem of explosive drones. He also vowed to prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons.

headlines.snippet: Sources indicate that the current round of US-led talks between Israel and Lebanon is the least productive to date. Both sides are reportedly displeased with the US-Iran deal's implications for Lebanon.

headlines.title: Defense Ministry civilian contractor killed in Gaza building collapse
headlines.snippet: Raad Abu al-Qi'an, a civilian contractor, was killed in a building collapse in Gaza. He is the first civilian contractor to die in the Strip since the ceasefire with Hamas.

headlines.snippet: Polls suggest a decline in support for the Bennett-Lapid alliance, while Eisenkot's party shows growth. Various potential party mergers are not predicted to significantly alter the electoral landscape.

headlines.snippet: Haredi convoys caused clashes and traffic disruptions, with most protesters unable to reach the IDF prison. Videos show public altercations, and a pregnant woman was reportedly injured.

headlines.snippet: Colombia's president-elect, Trump-backed De la Espriella, pledged to work closely with Israel, reversing the outgoing government's policies and strengthening ties with Washington and Jerusalem.

headlines.snippet: Israel's military strategy has been unsuccessful, but Netanyahu's opponents have not publicly acknowledged this, according to an analysis.

headlines.snippet: Iran has rejected the IAEA's access to damaged nuclear sites, stating that discussions will only occur after a final agreement and sanctions removal.

headlines.snippet: Iran's Oil Minister, Mohsen Paknejad, departed for India to attend the BRICS energy summit, with bilateral talks on energy cooperation scheduled.
